text: The distal rectal cuff is visible through the anal port following a transanal total mesorectal excision.

The surgeon places a purse string suture around the circumference of the distal rectal cuff through the anal port, using either standard surgical instruments or laparoscopic instruments. The suture ends are left untied at this stage.

The surgeon inserts a standard ten French ReadyVac drain through the center of the purse string suture on the distal rectal cuff, advancing it into the abdominal cavity. Once the drain is visible within the abdominal cavity, the purse string suture is knotted tightly around the drain.

The surgeon feeds the spindle of a 28mm circular stapler into the distal end of the drain, advancing the drain through the purse string into the abdominal cavity. The spindle of the stapler is guided through the center of the purse string.

The laparoscopic operator visualizes the spindle of the circular stapler fully within the abdominal cavity before removing the drain from the spindle. The stapled anastomosis is formed, ensuring the anal port is removed prior to closing the stapler.
The surgeon positions the circular stapler accurately through the purse string suture on the distal rectal cuff, preparing for a stapled anastomosis during the transanal total mesorectal excision.
